source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE:

At Omaze, we give our community in the UK and Germany the chance to win luxury homes and other life-changing prizes — all while raising money for the causes they love.



Thanks to our Omaze Community, we’ve raised over £100 million for UK charities in just five years. That’s millions helping organisations like Age UK, the RSPCA, British Heart Foundation and Great Ormond Street Hospital Charity deliver life-saving work.



And the best part? We’re only just getting started. Omaze is building a business and culture committed to growth and creating significant social impact on a global scale.




WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING AT OMAZE:

 - Growth: Omaze is one of the fastest-growing companies in the world.

 - Impact: Join a team of world changers dedicated to creating a ripple effect of good.

 - Pioneering: Be part of something no one has done before.

 - Culture: We work hard, grow together, and spread joy along the way.




🔑 ABOUT THE JOB

As a Senior Backend Engineer at Omaze, you are an experienced engineer who takes genuine ownership of your code, your decisions, and the outcomes they produce. Backend engineering is your specialism: you're at your best designing systems, building APIs, and making infrastructure decisions that hold up in high traffic products. You also have enough range across the stack to contribute wherever the team needs you and the pragmatism to know when to support your team. You embrace AI-assisted and agentic coding as part of how modern engineering gets done and apply it with the same judgement and rigour you bring to everything else. You believe everyone has something to contribute, value diversity and inclusion, and enjoy mentoring others to help them grow.



🔧 What You’ll Be Doing

 - Own the design, implementation, and testing of backend systems, APIs, and infrastructure producing code that's robust, well-considered, and built to operate reliably at scale.

 - Work closely with product, UX, and engineering peers from the earliest stages of a feature, shaping architecture decisions before they're hard to change.

 - Architect and build reusable, well-tested components that make the platform stronger and the next feature easier to ship.

 - Hold a high bar in code review giving feedback that's direct, constructive, and focused on design and long term maintainability, not just correctness.

 - Take responsibility for the performance, security, integrity, and resilience of what you build; raise concerns early and follow through on them.

 - Uphold and actively contribute to engineering standards and best practices, not because you're told to, but because you understand why they matter.

 - Use AI-assisted and agentic coding tools as a natural part of your workflow — knowing when they add leverage, when to interrogate their output, and how to review AI-generated code with the same rigour you'd apply to anything else.
   
   

🤩 About You

 - 7+ years of software engineering experience, with a strong backend background

 - Highly proficient with our server side tech stack: Typescript, DynamoDB, AWS Lambda, API Gateway and AWS CDK,

 - Experience with our client side tech stack: TypeScript and React.

 - Experience designing and building products or major features from concept to launch.

 - Highly proficient in unit, integration and end-to-end testing.

 - Comfortable operating in environments subject to regulatory, compliance, and risk-based security requirements.

 - Effective communicator in teams of technical and non-technical individuals.

 - You engage engineers to support a culture of collaboration and inclusion through mentorship, respectful code review, and dedication to quality.

 - You are excited to share and learn from your teammates about crafting fantastic user experiences.

 - You are passionate about voicing your opinions and can be humble in receiving feedback.

 - You are energised by sharing knowledge, learn constantly, and are unafraid to learn and iterate on your produc

source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:
At Omaze, we give our community in the UK and Germany the chance to win luxury homes and other life-changing prizes — all while raising money for the causes they love.

Thanks to our Omaze Community, we’ve raised over £100 million for UK charities in just five years. That’s millions helping organisations like Age UK, the RSPCA, British Heart Foundation and Great Ormond Street Hospital Charity deliver life-saving work.

And the best part? We’re only just getting started. Omaze is building a business and culture committed to growth and creating significant social impact on a global scale.



Why You’ll Love Working at Omaze:

 - Growth: Omaze is one of the fastest-growing companies in the world.

 - Impact: Join a team of world changers dedicated to creating a ripple effect of good.

 - Pioneering: Be part of something no one has done before.

 - Culture: We work hard, grow together, and spread joy along the way.



🔑 About The Job

As a Senior Software Engineer at Omaze, you are an experienced engineer who takes genuine ownership of your code, your decisions, and the outcomes they produce. Whilst being T-shaped with a frontend focus you're also comfortable across the stack and you don't wait to be told where to add value. You embrace AI-assisted and agentic coding as part of how modern engineering gets done and apply it with the same judgement and rigour you bring to everything else. You believe everyone has something to contribute, value diversity and inclusion, and enjoy mentoring others to help them grow.


🔧 What You’ll Be Doing

 - Own the design, implementation, and testing of features and services across the full stack, producing code that's clean, well-considered, and built to last.

 - Work closely with product, UX, and engineering peers from the earliest stages of a feature, not just at the point of implementation, shaping decisions before they're hard to change.

 - Architect and build reusable, well tested components and services that make the platform stronger and the next feature easier to ship.

 - Hold a high bar in code review, giving feedback that's direct, constructive, and focused on design and long term maintainability, not just correctness.

 - Take responsibility for the security, integrity, and resilience of what you build; raise concerns early and follow through on them.

 - Uphold and actively contribute to engineering standards and best practices, not because you're told to, but because you understand why they matter.

 - Use AI-assisted and agentic coding tools as a natural part of your workflow, knowing when they add leverage, when to interrogate their output, and how to review AI-generated code with the same rigour you'd apply to anything else.
   
   

🤩 About You

 - 7+ years of software engineering experience, with a strong full stack background and deep expertise in front end engineering.

 - Highly proficient with our client-side tech stack: Typescript, React, Next.js http://next.js and headless architecture.

 - Experience with some of our server-side tech stack: Typescript, DynamoDB, AWS Lambda, API Gateway, and AWS CDK.

 - Experience designing and building products or major features from concept to launch.

 - Highly proficient in unit, integration and end-to-end testing.

 - Comfortable operating in environments subject to regulatory, compliance, and risk-based security requirements.

 - Effective communicator in teams of technical and non-technical individuals.

 - You engage engineers to support a culture of collaboration and inclusion through mentorship, respectful code review, and dedication to quality.

 - You are excited to share and learn from your teammates about crafting fantastic user experiences.

 - You are passionate about voicing your opinions and can be humble in receiving feedback.

 - You are energised by sharing knowledge, learn constantly, and are unafraid to learn and iterate on your products quickly.
   
   

🙌What’s In It For You

 - Generous stock option

source_jobs.title: Content Lead Germany
source_jobs.description:
Omaze is reinventing fundraising by offering people the chance to win once-in-a-lifetime prizes while raising millions for charities around the world. Since launching in the UK in 2020, we've become one of the fastest-growing companies in the country and now, we’re scaling our impact across Europe.

We launched in Germany in June 2025 with ambitious growth goals and a clear mission: raise significant funds for local charities while delivering unforgettable experiences to our customers. In the UK alone, we've raised nearly £100 million for charity, and created over 40 new millionaires.

Now, we’re building a standout team in Germany – and we need your help to do it.



Learn more at omaze.de http://omaze.de.



🔑 About The Job

We’re looking for a Content Lead (DE) to take ownership of written content across all marketing channels. You will ensure that the Omaze brand voice is consistent, compelling, and localized for the German market in aligment with the Brand Positioning for Germany.

This is a hands-on role with strategic oversight and execution responsibilities. You will lead copywriting efforts, ensuring marketing copy across digital, email, website, brand and performance marketing assets. Additionally, you will build and manage a team of in-house and freelance copywriters, ensuring high-quality content production at scale.

If you have a passion for storytelling, excellent copywriting skills, and experience managing content teams, this role is for you.



🔧 What You’ll Be Doing

 - Content Strategy & Execution
   
   - Manage and further develop our content playbook for the German market, ensuring messaging resonates with local audiences.
   
   - Own the creation and management of marketing copy across web, email, digital, social media, TV scripts, and PR content.
   
   - Ensure all content is optimised for brand consistency, engagement, and conversion.

 - Copywriting & Editing
   
   - Write and refine engaging content for landing pages, email marketing, paid media, and social media ads tailored to the German market.
   
   - Proofread and edit copy from internal and external writers to maintain brand integrity and tone of voice.
   
   - Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements, such as gambling and advertising laws in Germany.

 - Team Leadership & Resource Management
   
   - Build and manage a team of in-house and freelance copywriters, scaling resources as needed to meet content demands.
   
   - Develop guidelines, workflows, and quality control processes to ensure high content standards.
   
   - Provide direction, training, and feedback to writers, ensuring alignment with brand tone of voice and performance objectives.
   
   - Balance hands-on content creation with managing and delegating workload effectively.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration
   
   - Work closely with the Marketing Director, Growth, Creative, Social Media, and Brand teams to align content with campaign objectives.
   
   - Collaborate with charity partners and media agencies to ensure content meets their branding and messaging needs.
   
   - Assist in research and performance tracking to refine content strategy based on customer engagement data.

🫵 About You

 - Fluent German speaker with excellent proficiency in English.

 - Strong copywriting and content creation experience, ideally in a startup, e-commerce, or performance-driven marketing environment.

 - Proven experience in building and managing content teams, including both in-house and freelance resources.

 - Ability to translate and adapt content from English to German while preserving tone and effectiveness.

 - Experience working with digital marketing teams and familiarity with content performance metrics.

 - Detail-oriented, organized, and capable of managing multiple projects under tight deadlines.

 - Passion for storytelling and a data-driven approach to content optimization.

 - Understanding of SEO principles, email marketing, and social media best practices is a

source_jobs.title: Property Director
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:
At Omaze, we give our community in the UK and Germany the chance to win luxury homes and other life-changing prizes — all while raising money for the causes they love.

Thanks to our Omaze Community, we’ve raised over £100 million for UK charities in just five years. That’s millions helping organisations like Age UK, the RSPCA, British Heart Foundation and Great Ormond Street Hospital Charity deliver life-saving work.

And the best part? We’re only just getting started. Omaze is building a business and culture committed to growth and creating significant social impact on a global scale.




🔑 ABOUT THE JOB

We're seeking an experienced and commercially minded Property Director to lead our property operations across the UK. In this role, you'll oversee the acquisition, management, and delivery of Omaze’s million-pound homes, ensuring that every Omaze home is a dream property worthy of our winners and the charitable causes we support.




🔧 WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

• Play a supporting role in building and developing Omaze's property pipeline, short-listing high-quality properties that align with customer expectations, brand strategy and campaign ambitions.

• Travel across the UK as required to personally view and evaluate properties, ensuring they meet Omaze's standards for quality, aesthetics, and marketability before progressing through the acquisition process.

• Oversee development projects to ensure they remain on schedule and within budget.

• Oversee all aspects of property operations, management, and maintenance, ensuring every home is maintained to the highest standard.

• Develop, implement, and continuously improve property management processes, standards, and procedures.

• Build and manage a trusted network of contractors, suppliers, and property professionals to support maintenance, refurbishment, and preparation activities as needed.

• Manage property budgets with rigour and commercial acumen, driving cost-effective operations without compromising on quality.

• Manage and develop members of the Property team who support the company’s property acquisition and management work.

• Work closely and collaboratively with the legal and finance teams on all aspects of property transactions, including conveyancing, title reviews, and contract negotiations.

• Manage and review all property due diligence with a commercial eye, including commissioning and reviewing surveys, valuations, and plans.

• Ensure all properties are acquired and managed in full compliance with UK property regulations, planning requirements, and relevant legal frameworks.

• Provide regular, clear reporting on property operations to the VP, Property & Partnerships

• Stay current on UK property market trends and best practices




🤩 ABOUT YOU

• 7+ years of experience within the property industry

• Calm under pressure and able to manage competing priorities with pragmatism and good judgement.

• A sharp, instinctive understanding of what makes a property highly marketable and an awareness of what looks exceptional on camera and translates to compelling visual content.

• Demonstrated experience identifying, managing, and coordinating contractors and suppliers to deliver high-quality property outcomes on time and within budget.

• Strong working knowledge of the conveyancing processes, and compliance requirements, with the ability to work effectively alongside legal and finance professionals.

• Proven ability to review and interpret property surveys, valuations, contractor budgets, and technical reports, and to translate findings into clear, actionable strategic advice.

• Excellent project management skills with the ability to manage multiple properties and workstreams simultaneously, without losing attention to detail.

• Budget management experience and a commercially astute mindset.

• Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills. Comfortable operating at senior level and managing cross-functional relationships.

• Ful

source_jobs.title: Performance Marketing Director
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:
At Omaze, we give our community in the UK and Germany the chance to win luxury homes and other life-changing prizes — all while raising money for the causes they love.

Thanks to our Omaze Community, we’ve raised over £100 million for UK charities in just five years. That’s millions helping organisations like Age UK, the RSPCA, British Heart Foundation and Great Ormond Street Hospital Charity deliver life-saving work.

And the best part? We’re only just getting started. Omaze is building a business and culture committed to growth and creating significant social impact on a global scale.



Why You’ll Love Working at Omaze:

 - Growth: Omaze is one of the fastest-growing companies in the world.

 - Impact: Join a team of world changers dedicated to creating a ripple effect of good.

 - Pioneering: Be part of something no one has done before.

 - Culture: We work hard, grow together, and spread joy along the way.




🔑 ABOUT THE JOB

We’re looking for a Performance Marketing Director to take our customer acquisition engine to the next level. In this role, you’ll lead Omaze’s global performance marketing strategy—owning growth across all paid channels and driving measurable business impact. Reporting to the VP of Growth, you'll play a key role in scaling our marketing efforts as we grow our audience and expand internationally.

You’ll bring a strong mix of strategic thinking and hands-on experience, with a focus on building scalable programs, improving efficiency, and uncovering new growth opportunities. Working closely with our Product, Analytics, Creative, and Brand teams, you’ll ensure our campaigns are optimized end-to-end—from first click to conversion. This is a great opportunity for someone who thrives in a fast-paced, high-growth environment and wants to combine performance with purpose.




🔧 WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

 - Develop and execute comprehensive performance marketing strategies across ATL, paid social, paid search, programmatic, affiliate, and emerging channels.

 - Lead and manage a high-performing team and agency partners.

 - Own customer acquisition targets and optimize LTV to CAC across all paid media efforts.

 - Work cross-functionally with Creative, Product, Data, and Brand teams to build high-converting campaigns and landing pages.

 - Leverage data and analytics to inform decision-making, run A/B tests, and iterate on strategies to improve CAC and LTV.

 - Monitor industry trends and emerging platforms to keep Omaze ahead of the curve in digital marketing innovation.

 - Establish scalable processes for campaign planning, execution, reporting, and budget management.




🤩 ABOUT YOU

 - Demonstrable performance marketing experience, ideally in e-commerce, scale-ups, or digital consumer brands

 - Proven success managing large budgets across multiple paid media channels with a track record of driving efficient growth.

 - Deep knowledge of digital platforms (Meta, Google Ads, TikTok, YouTube, etc.) and tools.

 - Strong leadership skills with experience building and managing high-performing teams.

 - Analytical mindset with proficiency in interpreting data, dashboards, and reports to derive actionable insights.

 - Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, high-growth environment with a test-and-learn mentality.




🙌 WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU

 - Generous stock options scheme

 - 25 days annual leave PLUS Bank Holidays

 - Private medical and dental insurance

 - 9% employer pension contributions, when you contribute at least 2%

 - A generous personal learning and development budget each year to use on training courses, conferences and professional memberships

 - Personal equipment budget to work from home

 - Enhanced family leave policies

 - Life assurance of 4x your salary



DEI Statement

We actively seek out diversity of thought and experience to drive innovation. We welcome all backgrounds, identities, and perspectives and work hard to ensure that every Omaze employee can bring their authenti

source_jobs.title: Staff Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
🌍 Location: Holborn, London

⭐ Office Policy: 3 days in person, 2 days at home each week



Who We Are:
At Omaze, we give our community in the UK and Germany the chance to win luxury homes and other life-changing prizes — all while raising money for the causes they love.

Thanks to our Omaze Community, we’ve raised over £100 million for UK charities in just five years. That’s millions helping organisations like Age UK, the RSPCA, British Heart Foundation and Great Ormond Street Hospital Charity deliver life-saving work.

And the best part? We’re only just getting started. Omaze is building a business and culture committed to growth and creating significant social impact on a global scale.




🔑 ABOUT THE JOB

As a Staff Data Engineer at Omaze, you will be a key member of Omaze’s lean data function. Reporting into the Director of Data Platforms, you're at your best designing pipelines, modelling foundational datasets, and embedding quality and observability into infrastructure that people genuinely trust. You bring strong judgement to architectural decisions, raise the bar through example, and create leverage for analytics engineers, data scientists, and stakeholders across Omaze. You embrace AI-assisted and agentic coding as part of how modern engineering gets done and apply it with the same judgement and rigour you bring to everything else




🔧 WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

 - Build and maintain robust ingestion and processing pipelines from our product systems, third-party tools, and external partners, using modern, well-tested code that holds up as the business changes

 - Build the data quality, observability, and lineage frameworks that make decision-critical datasets trustworthy, and make problems easy to find and fix when they happen

 - Work with engineers across the organisation to ensure that events are properly constructed, with the right parameters and nomenclature, so that the data flowing into our platform is trustworthy by design, not by accident (potentially through data contracts)

 - Ensure that data pipelines to external sources (e.g. paid marketing channels such as Meta) are reliably sharing appropriate, compliant data

 - Lay the foundations to support data science and ML work as it grows, e.g. feature pipelines, training datasets, and the platform capabilities that make those things easy when we want them

 - Make the cost of running our data platform visible and well understood, pushing for efficiency in the places it makes sense, and bringing judgement to when ongoing spend is justified by the value it returns

 - Generally take responsibility for data governance and compliance, ensuring that how we collect, store, and use data is responsible, secure, and meets the regulatory bar Omaze operates at

 - Use AI-assisted and agentic coding tools as a natural part of your workflow; knowing when they add leverage, when to interrogate their output, and how to review AI-generated code with the same rigour you'd apply to anything else.




🤩 ABOUT YOU

 - Extensive data engineering experience, with extensive experience across industry-standard data warehousing software (e.g. Databricks, Snowflake, etc.) and server-side tech (e.g. Typescript, DynamoDB, AWS Lambda, API Gateway and AWS CDK)

 - Hands-on experience with modern orchestration tooling (Aiflow, Dagster, etc.), ingestion tooling such as Fivetran (and experience building out custom scripts when the right off-the-shelf connector doesn’t exist)

 - A track record of building data quality, testing, and observability into platforms, not bolting them on afterwards

 - An understanding of data architecture strategies that can scale with the business for the next 4 years (not just the next 4 weeks!)

 - Experience supporting ML workflows / laying the data foundations for them

 - The ability to bar-raise through code review, mentorship, and the standards you set — not because you're told to, but because you understand why they matter




🙌 WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU

 - Gene

source_jobs.title: Senior Creative
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:
At Omaze, we give our community in the UK and Germany the chance to win luxury homes and other life-changing prizes — all while raising money for the causes they love.

Thanks to our Omaze Community, we’ve raised over £100 million for UK charities in just five years. That’s millions helping organisations like Age UK, the RSPCA, British Heart Foundation and Great Ormond Street Hospital Charity deliver life-saving work.

And the best part? We’re only just getting started. Omaze is building a business and culture committed to growth and creating significant social impact on a global scale.




🔑 ABOUT THE JOB

The Marketing team at Omaze is responsible for the growth and development of the UK business. As a Senior Creative, you will play a key role in bringing our brand to life across TV, digital, and social platforms.

Working closely with the Assistant Creative Director, you will lead the development and execution of high-quality, performance-driven creative, ensuring every output is impactful, on-brand, and delivered to the highest standard.




🔧 WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to create compelling, effective content that drives engagement and performance.

This role is ideal for a highly conceptual and hands-on creative with strong art direction skills and a passion for storytelling.




CREATIVE DEVELOPMENT & EXECUTION:

 - Conceptualise and deliver creative campaigns across TV, social and digital channels, with a focus on storytelling, engagement and performance

 - Translate creative briefs into high-quality, effective outputs that align with brand and commercial objectives

 - Partner closely with Brand, Social, Marketing and Production teams to bring bold, original campaign ideas to life, shaping the creative of key marketing initiatives.

 - Ensure consistency and integrity of the Omaze brand across all touchpoints

 - Push creative boundaries to deliver work that is not only high-performing but culturally resonant and distinctive.

 - Confident leading and contributing on set, working closely with directors, talent, and production teams to ensure creative is executed to a high standard.


ART DIRECTION & CRAFT EXCELLENCE:

 - Lead Art Direction across a range of outputs including TVCs, social content, and campaign assets

 - Own the creative quality of your workstreams, ensuring all assets meet a high bar for craft, storytelling and visual impact

 - Provide clear, constructive feedback to designers, editors, and external partners to elevate output

 - Remain hands-on in the creative process, including scripting, over seeing shoots and edit sessions where required.


TEAM COLLABORATION & SUPPORT:

 - Support the Assistant Creative Director in delivering the creative vision across campaigns

 - Act as a senior voice within the team, helping to guide and mentor agency creatives

 - Foster a collaborative, positive working environment that encourages creativity and continuous improvement


PROJECT OWNERSHIP & DELIVERY:

 - Own multiple projects end-to-end, ensuring work is delivered on time, on brief and to a high standard

 - Work closely with production teams and freelancers to deliver consistent, high-quality outputs

 - Manage timelines effectively and flag risks early to ensure smooth delivery




🤩 ABOUT YOU

 - Proven experience in a senior creative role within a fast-paced creative or marketing environment

 - Strong art direction and conceptual thinking skills, with a portfolio demonstrating high-quality execution across film and digital

 - Highly craft-focused, with a sharp eye for detail and storytelling

 - Confident working across TV and social content, with an understanding of performance-driven creative

 - Brings a strong understanding of editing processes and YouTube/social best practices, enabling them to guide teams in creating, iterating, and optimising content for engagement and performance.

 - Collaborative, proactive, and solutions-orient

source_jobs.title: Senior Business Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Omaze is reinventing fundraising by offering people the chance to win once-in-a-lifetime prizes while raising millions for charities around the world. Since launching in the UK in 2020, we've become one of the fastest-growing companies in the country and now, we’re scaling our impact across Europe.

We launched in Germany in June 2025 with ambitious growth goals and a clear mission: raise significant funds for local charities while delivering unforgettable experiences to our customers. In the UK alone, we've raised nearly £100 million for charity, and created over 40 new millionaires.

Now, we’re building a standout team in Germany – and we need your help to do it.

Learn more at omaze.de http://omaze.de.



🔑 About The Job

This is a cornerstone hire for a brand-new Business & Commercial Analytics function. As a Senior Business Analyst at Omaze, you will be a key member of Omaze’s lean data function. Reporting into the VP, Head of Data and Analytics, you'll be the analytical engine behind how Omaze understands and grows its commercial performance owning day-to-day business and commercial analytics across Omaze’s German business, supporting prizing, marketing, and growth. You will work hands-on every day, partnering directly with senior leaders across our German and UK teams, creating a unified 360-degree view of our customers, interrogating subscription and pay as you go (PAYG) behaviour, and turning complex data into clear, confident commercial decisions.

You will also set the bar, the methods we trust, the metrics we agree on, and the pitfalls we never repeat. Turning cohort retention curves and survival models into clear views on which behaviours, segments and journeys drive durable value will be your day-to-day, and building advanced behavioral segmentation that the company can act on is how you will drive significant value across the company. It's a high-visibility role with ownership and freedom to shape how commercial analytics is done at Omaze in Germany.



🔧 What You’ll Be Doing

Own Customer Analytics Across Germany

 - Develop a comprehensive view of customer behaviour across web and mobile experiences.

 - Define lifecycle stages, customer segments, and key LTV drivers across both subscription and pay-as-you-go customers.

 - Build a deeper understanding of what drives acquisition, engagement, retention, and long-term value.

Drive Commercial Performance

 - Lead pricing, margin, contribution, and profitability analysis.

 - Identify commercial opportunities through customer, product, and cohort analysis.

 - Support decision-making with clear recommendations grounded in data.

Lead Retention & Lifecycle Analytics

 - Build retention models, churn diagnostics, survival analyses, and customer forecasting models.

 - Identify drivers of retention and develop actionable strategies to improve customer lifetime value.

 - Analyse dormant user behaviour and reactivation opportunities.

Improve Measurement & Experimentation

 - Help define and evolve Omaze Germany's measurement framework and key performance metrics.

 - Apply advanced causal inference techniques when traditional A/B testing isn't possible, including:
   
   - Difference-in-differences
   
   - Synthetic controls
   
   - Propensity modelling
   
   - Instrumental variables
   
   - Geo-based experiments

Forecast & Influence Strategic Decisions

 - Build revenue forecasts, scenario models, and opportunity-sizing frameworks.

 - Support planning across draw cycles, marketing investment, and commercial strategy.

 - Bring data-driven insight into prioritisation discussions with senior stakeholders.

Champion Analytics Excellence

 - Ensure robust tracking and instrumentation across customer journeys.

 - Conduct proactive deep-dives and root-cause analyses to identify risks and opportunities.

 - Help establish best practices, analytical standards, and a culture of evidence-based decision-making across the business.
   
   

🫵 About You

We're looking fo

source_jobs.title: Contract Designer (4 months)
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:
At Omaze, we give our community in the UK and Germany the chance to win luxury homes and other life-changing prizes — all while raising money for the causes they love.

Thanks to our Omaze Community, we’ve raised over £100 million for UK charities in just five years. That’s millions helping organisations like Age UK, the RSPCA, British Heart Foundation and Great Ormond Street Hospital Charity deliver life-saving work.

And the best part? We’re only just getting started. Omaze is building a business and culture committed to growth and creating significant social impact on a global scale.




🔑 ABOUT THE JOB

We're looking for a talented Design Consultant to join Omaze on a contract basis, to play a meaningful role in one of the most exciting things we're working on right now — our brand evolution. This will be an inside IR35 contract.

This isn't a catch-all design role. It's a focused opportunity to take a beautiful set of atomic components and craft them into pages that genuinely perform — pages that feel unmistakably Omaze and convert. You'll be working closely with some of the most senior people in the business, and with a marketing team that cares deeply about every pixel of the brand experience. If you love the craft of UI, get excited by conversion problems, and know how to bring a room full of strong opinions to a clear direction — this one's for you.




🔧 WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

 - Take our atomic design components and build them into comprehensive, conversion-optimised web pages that bring our brand evolution to life

 - Own the overhaul of our core conversion pages, making sure every touchpoint feels like a cohesive, premium Omaze experience from first impression to final click

 - Work hand-in-hand with our marketing team to ensure the design and brand story land consistently across the full customer journey

 - Present and champion design direction with exec and senior leadership to drive alignment— running rapid feedback and iteration cycles with confidence, cutting through the noise, and keeping the vision on track

 - Help design and support A/B testing so that every change we make is backed by evidence, not just instinct

 - Collaborate with the wider design team to grow and evolve our component library as a natural output of the pages you create

 - Partner closely with engineers to bring designs into Storybook, helping to build a front-end component system we're all proud of




🤩 ABOUT YOU

 - Our ideal candidate has extensive experience as a Product Designer, Brand Designer, or similar, with a portfolio that shows real UI craft — ideally across marketing or conversion-focused web pages

 - Hands-on experience with atomic design systems, taking components and composing them into polished, production-ready pages

 - A track record of driving alignment around design with exec and senior stakeholders — you can hold a room, earn trust, and guide a group to a decision

 - A genuine interest in performance — you understand how design choices move metrics, and you're comfortable contributing to A/B testing programmes

 - Experience working closely with marketing teams, with an eye for brand coherence from campaign through to conversion

 - Familiarity with design systems and Storybook, and experience collaborating with engineers to translate design into a component library

 - Figma expertise — including building with and extending component libraries

 - Strong communication skills and the kind of presence that makes stakeholders feel confident in the direction you're taking them



DEI Statement

We actively seek out diversity of thought and experience to drive innovation. We welcome all backgrounds, identities, and perspectives and work hard to ensure that every Omaze employee can bring their authentic self to work at all times.

source_jobs.title: AI Enablement Specialist
source_jobs.description:
⭐ Office Policy: 3 days in person, 2 days at home each week

📝 Contract type: Permanent, full time

💬 Language Requirements: German and English business fluent



Omaze is reinventing fundraising by offering people the chance to win once-in-a-lifetime prizes while raising millions for charities around the world. Since launching in the UK in 2020, we've become one of the fastest-growing companies in the country and now, we’re scaling our impact across Europe.

We launched in Germany in June 2025 with ambitious growth goals and a clear mission: raise significant funds for local charities while delivering unforgettable experiences to our customers. In the UK alone, we've raised nearly £100 million for charity, and created over 40 new millionaires.

Now, we’re building a standout team in Germany – and we need your help to do it.

Learn more at omaze.de http://omaze.de.



🔑 About The Job

As our AI Enablement Specialist, you will be the first embedded member of Omaze’s AI Operations team in Germany.

Working closely with teams across the business, you will identify opportunities to improve workflows, reduce manual effort, and accelerate delivery through AI, automation, and smarter ways of working. You will act as the bridge between our global AI Operations team and local stakeholders, helping teams adopt modern tools, embrace new ways of working, and deliver measurable business impact.

This is not a traditional engineering role. While you should be comfortable building automations and prototypes, your success will be defined by your ability to understand business problems, influence stakeholders, drive adoption, and help teams work smarter.

You will report into the Global AI Operations team and work closely with colleagues across Germany and the UK to support Omaze’s broader AI transformation journey.



🔧 What You’ll Be Doing

Understand Business Workflows

 - Partner with teams across Germany to understand workflows, challenges, bottlenecks, and opportunities for improvement.

 - Facilitate discovery sessions and map current processes.

 - Identify opportunities where AI, automation, or better tooling can create measurable impact.

 - Act as a trusted advisor to stakeholders across the business.

Enable AI Adoption

 - Support teams in adopting AI-enabled tools and workflows already being rolled out globally.

 - Deliver training, workshops, and coaching sessions that build confidence and capability.

 - Create practical guidance, playbooks, and examples that help teams integrate AI into their day-to-day work.

 - Champion responsible experimentation and continuous learning.

Build & Prototype Solutions

 - Design and implement automations, workflows, and AI-enabled solutions.

 - Rapidly prototype ideas using AI tools, no-code/low-code platforms, and workflow automation softwares.

 - Work with the wider AI Operations team to scale successful local initiatives into broader business solutions.

 - Measure outcomes and continuously improve implementations.

Drive Operational Improvement

 - Identify opportunities to simplify processes and remove manual work.

 - Support high-impact initiatives across functions including production, operations, marketing, customer experience, and corporate teams.

 - Help teams focus on higher-value work by reducing repetitive administrative effort.

 - Contribute to larger global AI transformation programmes alongside the UK AI team.

Act as the AI Team Representative in Germany

 - Serve as the local face of the AI Operations team.

 - Build strong relationships across the organisation and proactively surface opportunities, challenges, and feedback.

 - Bring local insights and opportunities back to the global AI team to influence priorities and future initiatives.

 - Help ensure successful adoption of new tools and ways of working without becoming the long-term owner of those tools
   
   

🫵 About You

 - Naturally curious and passionate about understanding how businesses operate, identifyi

source_jobs.title: Public Relationship Manager - Germany
source_jobs.description:
Omaze is reinventing fundraising by offering people the chance to win once-in-a-lifetime prizes while raising millions for charities around the world. Since launching in the UK in 2020, we've become one of the fastest-growing companies in the country and now, we’re scaling our impact across Europe.

We launched in Germany in June 2025 with ambitious growth goals and a clear mission: raise significant funds for local charities while delivering unforgettable experiences to our customers. In the UK alone, we've raised nearly £100 million for charity, and created over 40 new millionaires.

Now, we’re building a standout team in Germany – and we need your help to do it.

Learn more at omaze.de http://omaze.de.



🔑 About The Job

As our Public Relationship Manager for Germany, you'll shape how Omaze is seen across one of our most exciting growth markets. You'll partner with our local leadership team to shape our public relations strategy, building awareness, trust and excitement around the Omaze brand, our luxury house draws and the incredible charitable impact we create together with our community.

You'll develop compelling stories around our winners, build strong relationships with journalists, influencers and key stakeholders, and deliver campaigns that generate high-quality earned media. Working closely with Marketing, Brand, Social, Creative and Charity Partnerships, you'll ensure every campaign reaches the widest possible audience and strengthens Omaze's reputation in Germany.

This is an opportunity for someone who thrives on combining strategic thinking with hands-on execution and wants to make a genuine social impact while helping grow an ambitious international brand.



🔧 What You’ll Be Doing

Develop our PR strategy

 - Further develop and own our Omaze Germany's PR and communications strategy.

 - Create integrated communications plans that support business, brand and commercial objectives.

 - Identify opportunities to position Omaze as an innovative entertainment and purpose-driven brand.

Deliver standout campaigns

 - Lead PR activity for house launches, winner stories, charity partnerships and corporate announcements for national and local audiences

 - Develop engaging press materials, media pitches, thought leadership and executive communications.

 - Work closely with Creative and Marketing teams to maximise campaign impact across earned, owned and shared channels.

Build strong media relationships

 - Develop and maintain excellent relationships with national, regional and lifestyle media across Germany.

 - Proactively secure high-quality media coverage and manage incoming press enquiries.

 - Identify opportunities with influencers, creators and brand ambassadors where appropriate.

Protect and strengthen our reputation

 - Monitor media coverage, sentiment and emerging issues.

 - Support crisis communications planning and execution when required.

 - Ensure messaging remains consistent, accurate and aligned across all communications.

Measure success

 - Track PR performance using meaningful KPIs including reach, quality of coverage, share of voice and brand sentiment.

 - Provide regular reporting and actionable insights to improve future campaigns.
   
   

🫵 About You

You'll be an experienced communications professional who knows how to turn great stories into national headlines.

You'll bring:

 - Significant experience in public relations, corporate communications or brand communications, preferably in a D2C type environment

 - A proactive, creative and highly organised approach with the ability to manage multiple priorities.

 - Experience working in Germany with an excellent understanding of the German media landscape.

 - Strong relationships with journalists across consumer, lifestyle, business and national media.

 - Outstanding written and verbal communication skills in both German and English.

 - Experience developing integrated PR campaigns that deliver measurable business results.

source_jobs.title: Media Production Project Manager
source_jobs.description:
Omaze is reinventing fundraising by offering people the chance to win once-in-a-lifetime prizes while raising millions for charities around the world. Since launching in the UK in 2020, we've become one of the fastest-growing companies in the country and now, we’re scaling our impact across Europe.

We launched in Germany in June 2025 with ambitious growth goals and a clear mission: raise significant funds for local charities while delivering unforgettable experiences to our customers. In the UK alone, we've raised nearly £100 million for charity, and created over 40 new millionaires.

Now, we’re building a standout team in Germany – and we need your help to do it.

Learn more at omaze.de http://omaze.de.




🔑 ABOUT THE JOB

At Omaze, creativity moves fast. From luxury home launches to fundraising campaigns, we create an incredible amount of content across multiple channels and that's where you come in.

As our Media Production/Delivery Project Manager, you'll be the driving force behind keeping our creative projects running smoothly from brief to delivery. You'll partner with Creative, Production, Marketing, Product and external partners to ensure every project is delivered on time, on budget and to an exceptional standard.

This isn't a traditional project management role. We're looking for someone who's excited about the future of creative operations and understands how AI can transform the way teams work. You'll champion smarter ways of planning, documenting and managing projects using Notion and AI-powered workflows, helping the business spend less time on administration and more time creating outstanding work.

You'll constantly look for opportunities to improve processes, automate repetitive tasks and build scalable systems that allow our creative teams to do their best work.






🔧 WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

Own Content Delivery

 - Own the end-to-end delivery of creative and content projects across multiple teams.

 - Build clear project plans, timelines and priorities while balancing competing deadlines.

 - Ensure projects move efficiently from briefing through production, approvals and delivery.

 - Identify risks early and proactively remove blockers before they impact delivery.

Optimise Creative Operations

 - Develop scalable workflows that improve how content is planned, produced and tracked.

 - Own and continuously improve our project management workspace in Notion.

 - Create templates, dashboards and documentation that make project information accessible and actionable.

 - Establish clear ways of working that improve collaboration across Creative, Marketing and Production.

Bring AI Into Everyday Project Management

 - Use AI to improve project planning, meeting notes, documentation, reporting and workflow management.

 - Design AI-enabled workflows within Notion to reduce manual administration and improve team efficiency.

 - Experiment with new AI capabilities and identify practical opportunities to improve how content is delivered.

 - Build repeatable prompts, templates and automations that help teams work smarter.

 - Partner with our AI Enablement team to embed new ways of working across the creative organisation.

Collaborate Across The Business

 - Work closely with Creative, Marketing, Product, Brand, Production and external agencies.

 - Keep stakeholders informed through clear communication and regular project updates.

 - Facilitate project kick-offs, planning sessions and retrospectives that drive continuous improvement.

 - Foster a culture of accountability, transparency and collaboration.

Measure & Improve

 - Monitor project performance and delivery metrics.

 - Use data to identify bottlenecks and recommend process improvements.

 - Continuously evolve our project management practices as the business grows.

You're an organised problem solver who loves bringing order to complexity. You enjoy helping creative people do their best work by creating systems that make work fee

source_jobs.title: Customer Service Manager
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:
At Omaze, we give our community in the UK and Germany the chance to win luxury homes and other life-changing prizes — all while raising money for the causes they love.

Thanks to our Omaze Community, we’ve raised over £100 million for UK charities in just five years. That’s millions helping organisations like Age UK, the RSPCA, British Heart Foundation and Great Ormond Street Hospital Charity deliver life-saving work.

And the best part? We’re only just getting started. Omaze is building a business and culture committed to growth and creating significant social impact on a global scale.




🔑 ABOUT THE JOB

As our Customer Service Manager, you’ll play a key role in delivering a seamless, high-quality customer experience across every interaction. You’ll lead and develop a small internal Customer Experience (CX) team, while overseeing a larger outsourced support function, ensuring we consistently meet performance targets across email, voice and live chat.

Working closely with senior leadership, you’ll contribute to shaping and refining our customer experience strategy, while taking ownership of its day-to-day execution and continuous optimisation.

You’ll be responsible for building scalable processes, improving tools and documentation, and enabling the team to respond efficiently as demand grows. Acting as the voice of the customer, you’ll surface insights, identify trends and work cross-functionally to improve the overall customer journey.




🔧 WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

 - Delivering against key service metrics across email, voice and live chat, including CSAT, First Time Resolution, SLAs and response times

 - Monitoring, analysing and reporting on service performance, using data to identify trends and drive continuous improvement

 - Leading, coaching and developing a small internal CX team, while managing the performance and output of a larger outsourced partner

 - Managing workload forecasting and resource planning to ensure effective coverage and cost efficiency across both in-house and outsourced teams

 - Collaborating with outsource partners to set daily service goals, manage performance and maintain quality standards

 - Building and maintaining a strong foundation of documentation, training and processes to support a lean and scalable support model

 - Acting as the voice of the customer by capturing and sharing insights with Product, Marketing and other teams to influence improvements

 - Supporting cross-functional initiatives to enhance customer experience through new tools, processes, policies and product changes

 - Partnering with senior leadership to contribute to the development and evolution of the customer experience strategy

 - Leading the CX response to incidents, coordinating with relevant teams and implementing new processes where required

 - Handling complex and escalated customer issues with empathy and sound judgement

 - Identifying and escalating risks or recurring issues impacting customer experience

 - Managing and supporting privacy rights processes in line with regulatory requirements

 - Running regular quality assurance and performance reviews with outsource partners

 - Partnering with senior leadership to support planning around budgets, service levels and customer satisfaction targets
   
   




🤩 ABOUT YOU

 - You have solid experience managing customer service or customer experience teams in a fast-paced environment

 - You have experience managing or working closely with outsourced support partners at scale

 - You’re confident working with performance metrics (CSAT, FTR, SLAs) and using data to drive decisions

 - You have hands-on experience across multiple support channels (email, voice and live chat)

 - You’re a strong people manager who enjoys coaching, developing and empowering teams

 - You’re operationally excellent, with a track record of improving processes, tools and ways of working

 - You’re highly organised, with experience in forecasting, planning and prio

source_jobs.title: Senior Performance Marketing Manager - ATL
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:
At Omaze, we give our community in the UK and Germany the chance to win luxury homes and other life-changing prizes all while raising money for the causes they love.

Thanks to our Omaze Community, we’ve raised over £100 million for UK charities in just five years. That’s millions helping organisations like Age UK, the RSPCA, British Heart Foundation and Great Ormond Street Hospital Charity deliver life-saving work.

And the best part? We’re only just getting started. Omaze is building a business and culture committed to growth and creating significant social impact on a global scale.




🔑 ABOUT THE JOB

We are looking for an experienced Senior Performance Marketing Manager to lead our global Above-the-Line (ATL) media strategy and drive sustainable business growth across international markets. This role is responsible for developing data-driven media strategies, managing agency partnerships, and ensuring fully integrated marketing campaigns that deliver measurable commercial outcomes. Working cross-functionally with Marketing, Analytics and Product, you will oversee end-to-end ATL planning, execution, measurement and optimisation.




🔧 WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

Lead Global Media Strategy

Define and execute Omaze's global ATL media strategy, ensuring investment supports commercial objectives and market growth.

 - Lead the development and execution of the global ATL media strategy, ensuring investment supports each market's commercial objectives and growth targets.

 - Translate business priorities into effective media strategies that balance brand building with measurable performance outcomes.

 - Identify new media opportunities, channels and market trends to drive competitive advantage.

Drive Integrated Marketing

 - Partner with Growth, Product, Brand and Creative teams to deliver fully integrated campaigns across paid, owned and earned channels.

 - Ensure ATL activity complements wider acquisition, retention and brand initiatives.

 - Align media investment with broader business priorities and product launches.

Own Media Planning & Agency Partnerships

 - Build strong relationships with global media agency partners to deliver best-in-class planning, buying and optimisation.

 - Review, challenge and approve media strategies and plans, ensuring they are aligned with business objectives, audience insights and commercial targets.

 - Lead commercial negotiations with agencies and key media partners to maximise value and effectiveness.

Optimise Marketing Investment

 - Own and manage significant ATL media budgets in partnership with Finance and Growth teams.

 - Develop investment scenarios based on commercial objectives, market conditions and growth opportunities.

 - Continuously optimise budget allocation using campaign performance, customer insights and business priorities.

 - Ensure marketing investment delivers maximum return whilst supporting long-term brand health.

Build Best-in-Class Measurement

 - Partner with Data and Analytics teams to develop robust measurement frameworks across all media activity.

 - Lead attribution strategy, Marketing Mix Modelling (MMM), incrementality testing and experimentation programmes.

 - Analyse campaign performance, media effectiveness and customer data to generate actionable insights that inform future planning.

 - Present performance updates, investment recommendations and commercial insights to senior leadership.

Collaborate on Creative Excellence

 - Work closely with Brand and Creative teams to develop high-performing campaign assets that maximise media effectiveness.

 - Produce clear creative briefs with defined objectives, success measures and delivery timelines.

 - Foster a culture of continuous creative testing and optimisation to improve campaign performance.

Lead & Influence

 - Act as the senior performance marketing expert across the organisation.

 - Influence executive stakeholders through data-driven recommendations and strategic thinking.

 -

source_jobs.title: People Operations Specialist
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE:

At Omaze, we give our community in the UK and Germany the chance to win luxury homes and other life-changing prizes — all while raising money for the causes they love.

Thanks to our Omaze Community, we’ve raised over £100 million for UK charities in just five years. That’s millions helping organisations like Age UK, the RSPCA, British Heart Foundation and Great Ormond Street Hospital Charity deliver life-saving work.

And the best part? We’re only just getting started. Omaze is building a business and culture committed to growth and creating significant social impact on a global scale.




🔑 ABOUT THE JOB

The People team at Omaze is evolving, and we're looking for someone who gets excited about building the systems, processes and automations that help a business scale.

You'll own the operational heartbeat of the People function, from employee lifecycle processes and HR systems to the annual people cycles that keep the business moving. You'll be the day-to-day owner of our HRIS during an exciting period of platform evolution, while continuously identifying smarter ways of working across the team.

We're looking for someone who thinks like a systems builder. When you spot a repetitive task, your instinct isn't to accept it, it's to automate it. You enjoy improving workflows, removing friction and using technology to make work better for everyone.

AI is already changing how great operations teams work, and we expect it to be part of your toolkit. Whether you're drafting documentation, building workflows or improving processes, you'll use technology to help us move faster and operate more effectively.

If you're passionate about creating scalable operations rather than simply maintaining them, we'd love to hear from you.




🔧 WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

Build operational excellence

 - Own the day-to-day operation of the People function, ensuring our processes are accurate, efficient and consistently deliver a great employee experience.

 - Manage the end-to-end employee lifecycle, from onboarding to offboarding, continually improving how these experiences are delivered.

 - Create, document and evolve People procedures so they're simple, scalable and easy for managers and employees to use.

 - Coordinate and support our annual performance review and pay review cycles.

Own and improve our People systems

 - Be the day-to-day owner of our HRIS, maintaining workflows, permissions, automations and data integrity.

 - Play a key role in our HRIS migration and future development, helping shape how we use technology to support our growing business.

 - Continuously review existing processes and identify opportunities to automate manual work across the People team.

Use technology to work smarter

 - Build and improve automated workflows, notifications and approvals that remove administrative burden.

 - Use AI tools to create first drafts of policies, communications, documentation and operational resources, improving speed and consistency.

 - Experiment with new technologies and recommend ways they can improve the employee experience and team effectiveness.




🤩 ABOUT YOU

You're someone who enjoys making organisations work better. You probably find yourself questioning why something is manual, looking for opportunities to simplify it, and getting genuine satisfaction from creating elegant operational solutions.

You'll bring:

 - Significant experience in a People Operations or People Systems role within a fast-moving organisation.

 - Confidence building workflows, automations, permissions and system configurations in a modern HRIS.

 - Excellent Google Sheets skills, including pivot tables, XLOOKUP/VLOOKUP and data analysis.

 - A strong understanding of employee lifecycle processes and how technology can improve them.

 - Excellent attention to detail with a passion for operational excellence.

 - Strong written communication skills and the ability to create clear, practical documentation.

 - Confidence using AI tool

source_jobs.title: Senior Performance Marketing Manager - Google/Youtube
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:
At Omaze, we give our community in the UK and Germany the chance to win luxury homes and other life-changing prizes — all while raising money for the causes they love.

Thanks to our Omaze Community, we’ve raised over £100 million for UK charities in just five years. That’s millions helping organisations like Age UK, the RSPCA, British Heart Foundation and Great Ormond Street Hospital Charity deliver life-saving work.

And the best part? We’re only just getting started. Omaze is building a business and culture committed to growth and creating significant social impact on a global scale.




🔑 ABOUT THE JOB

As our Senior Performance Marketing Manager (Google & YouTube), you'll own one of Omaze's most important growth engines, leading the strategy, investment and optimisation of Google's advertising ecosystem across Search, YouTube, Performance Max, Demand Gen and Display.

Working at the intersection of Growth, Creative, Product, CRM, Analytics and Finance, you'll translate commercial objectives into acquisition strategies that drive sustainable customer growth while maximising marketing efficiency.

This isn't simply a campaign management role. You'll shape investment strategy, influence senior stakeholders, develop measurement frameworks and continuously identify new opportunities to improve performance through Google's rapidly evolving AI-powered advertising products.

You'll combine strategic thinking with hands-on execution, ensuring every campaign delivers measurable business impact while helping millions more people discover Omaze.

Own Google's Growth Strategy

 - Lead the strategy, planning and execution of Google Ads across Search, YouTube, Performance Max, Demand Gen and Display.

 - Develop scalable full-funnel acquisition strategies that balance customer acquisition, revenue growth and long-term brand building.

 - Translate commercial objectives into media investment strategies that maximise business performance across multiple markets.

 - Manage significant Google media budgets, developing quarterly investment plans and continuously optimising spend based on commercial priorities.
